stem: Which directive adds or removes an element from the DOM based on a condition?
hint: One of these only toggles CSS display; the answer removes the element from the DOM entirely.
feedback: `v-if` actually adds/removes the element from the DOM. `v-show` keeps it mounted and only toggles CSS `display` — reach for `v-show` when you toggle often, `v-if` when the condition rarely changes.

stem: Which directive listens for DOM events such as click?
hint: Its shorthand is the @ symbol.
feedback: `v-on` (shorthand `@`) attaches event listeners — `@click`, `@input`, and so on. `v-bind` (shorthand `:`) binds attributes and props, not events.

stem: Which directive renders a list of elements from an array?
hint: You always pair it with a unique key.
feedback: `v-for` iterates arrays, objects, or ranges — `v-for="item in items"`. Always pair it with a unique `:key`.

stem: How does data flow through props between a parent and child component?
hint: A child can't reassign a prop — they're read-only.
feedback: Props form a one-way-down binding: the parent updates the child, never the reverse. To send data upward, emit an event.

stem: Which template syntax interpolates a reactive value as text?
hint: Vue calls it the mustache syntax.
feedback: Vue uses the mustache syntax `{{ }}` for text interpolation; whatever sits inside is evaluated as a JavaScript expression.

stem: Which directive creates a two-way binding on a form input?
hint: On a native input it's shorthand for a value bind plus an input listener.
feedback: `v-model` on a native input is sugar for `:value` + `@input`. `v-sync` was a Vue 2 modifier and was removed in Vue 3.

stem: How do you conditionally apply a CSS class in a template?
hint: It's an attribute binding, not its own directive.
feedback: `:class` (shorthand for `v-bind:class`) takes an object or array to toggle classes reactively. There is no `v-class` directive in Vue.

stem: Which element marks where a child renders content passed in by its parent?
hint: The child marks the hole; the parent fills it.
feedback: The child places a `<slot>` where parent-provided content should render. The parent fills the default slot, or a named one via `#name`.

stem: Which Composition API function creates a reactive reference to a primitive value?
hint: The reactive() function can't hold a bare number.
feedback: `ref` wraps any value — including primitives — and exposes it as `.value`. `reactive` only works on objects and can't hold a primitive directly.

stem: Which function creates cached state that derives from other reactive sources?
hint: It memoizes, recomputing only when a dependency changes.
feedback: `computed` caches its result and only re-evaluates when a dependency changes. `watch` runs side effects; it doesn't produce a cached value.

stem: By convention, what prefix do composable function names use?
hint: Think useMouse, useFetch, useStorage.
feedback: Composables are named with a `use` prefix — `useMouse`, `useFetch` — signalling they tap into Vue's reactivity and lifecycle.

stem: By default, a component's v-model binds the modelValue prop and emits which event?
hint: It desugars to a modelValue prop plus an update event.
feedback: In Vue 3, `v-model` on a component is sugar for `:modelValue` plus `@update:modelValue`. Named models like `v-model:title` use `update:title`.

stem: Which function runs a callback with the old and new value when a specific source changes?
hint: It hands you both the previous and current value.
feedback: `watch` observes a specific source and passes `(next, prev)` to its callback. `watchEffect` runs immediately and tracks dependencies automatically, with no old/new values.

stem: Which function makes an object deeply reactive with no .value wrapper?
hint: For objects, and you access properties directly.
feedback: `reactive` makes an object deeply reactive and is accessed without `.value`. `ref` is preferred for primitives and works for objects too when you want a single reassignable reference.

stem: Which Composition API hook runs after the component is inserted into the DOM?
hint: Composition API lifecycle hooks start with on.
feedback: `onMounted` fires once the component's DOM is in the page. `mounted`/`created` are Options API; there is no `onCreated` — `setup()` itself runs at that point.

stem: Which compiler macro declares a component's props in script setup?
hint: A macro you use without importing it.
feedback: `defineProps` is the `<script setup>` compiler macro for declaring props. `defineEmits` declares events and `defineModel` wires up `v-model`.

stem: Which API groups reactive effects so they can all be disposed together?
hint: Calling its stop() method disposes every watcher created inside it.
feedback: `effectScope()` captures the watchers/computeds created inside it; calling the scope's `.stop()` disposes them all at once — the backbone of reusable, self-cleaning composables.

stem: Which ref variant reacts only to reassigning .value, not to deep mutations of its contents?
hint: Deep mutations are ignored; only reassigning the value triggers it.
feedback: `shallowRef` tracks only `.value` reassignment — ideal for large or externally-managed objects where deep tracking is wasteful. Use `triggerRef` to force an update after an in-place change.

stem: Which function resolves after Vue has flushed pending DOM updates?
hint: Await it before measuring an element you just changed.
feedback: `nextTick()` returns a promise that resolves once the DOM reflects the latest state change — await it before measuring or reading updated elements.

stem: Which function creates virtual DOM nodes inside a render function?
hint: It's the one-letter function your templates compile down into.
feedback: `h()` (hyperscript) builds vnodes: `h('div', { class: 'x' }, children)`. It's what templates compile down to and what you write by hand in a render function.

stem: Which built-in component renders its children at a different place in the DOM?
hint: Perfect for modals that must escape an overflow-clipped parent.
feedback: `Teleport` moves its children to a target elsewhere in the DOM (often `body`) while keeping them logically in the component — ideal for modals and tooltips. `Portal` was its Vue 2 / React name.

stem: Which built-in component waits on async setup and shows fallback content while pending?
hint: It has a default slot and a fallback slot.
feedback: `Suspense` coordinates async dependencies — async `setup()` and async components — showing its `#fallback` slot until they resolve. It's still marked experimental.

stem: In SSR, what is the client-side step that makes server-rendered HTML interactive called?
hint: It attaches reactivity to HTML that already exists.
feedback: Hydration is the step where Vue attaches event listeners and reactivity to server-rendered markup instead of recreating the DOM — a mismatch between server and client output breaks it.

stem: Which watcher flush timing runs the callback after Vue has updated the DOM?
hint: The timing that waits for the DOM to settle first.
feedback: `flush: 'post'` runs the watcher after Vue flushes DOM updates, so you can read updated elements. `'pre'` (the default) runs before, and `'sync'` runs synchronously on every change.
